stemHtml: Which is an operational requirement concerning ice, snow, or frost on aircraft surfaces?

options.a: Take-off may be attempted with snow, ice or frost adhering to the wings, stabiliser or control surfaces provided they are smooth.
options.b: Take-off may be attempted if there is no ice on the leading edges, and less than 5 mm ice on the rest of the wing upper surfaces and control surfaces.
options.c: Take-off may be attempted with snow, ice or frost adhering to the wings, stabiliser or control surfaces provided they are smooth and anti-/de-icing equipment is in operation.
options.d: Take-off shall not be commenced unless the external surfaces are clear of any deposit which might adversely affect the performance and/or controllability of the aeroplane.
